• Across 1,153 chars of NextStep covering eight dated entries from Nov 2025 to Feb 2026 ‚Äî including CPO Luke's CFO presentation, Pronto upgrade wait, RFP template ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic or GenAI despite Merlin Intake, Agentic AI and ANA being in product scope. Per the 9 Feb 2026 entry: 'met with CPO, upgrade of Pronto is taking much longer than expected. Looking at reviewing Procurement in 6 months time.'
• Per the 24 Nov 2025 entry: 'Spoke to Luke, he presented to the CFO. Very happy with an S2P option. Coupa and Zycus are going to be considered. First they need to upgrade Pronto, and then clean their data in Pronto then will seek further info from both vendors. Will reach out at the back early 2026.' On 6 Nov 2025: 'We did a demo, and overview- he is joining our event online PlAn on 18th. Presenting us and Coupa to CFO in 1 week for further actions internally.' Coupa is the competitive comparison; Pronto ERP upgrade is the gating dependency.

Aust Homeware has the full AI loadout (Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) and CPO Luke is engaged with the CFO, but the deal is gated by Pronto ERP upgrade timing and the competitive frame is Coupa-vs-Zycus on core S2P. metrics.narrative: Luke Cerra articulated the business problem on 24 Nov 2025: they need to upgrade from manual procurement processes and a Pronto ERP system that lacks a procurement module, and they are seeking a full Source-to-Pay (S2P) solution. Luke presented the S2P concept to the CFO and was 'very happy with an S2P option.' However, no quantified business case has been documented. No ROI, cost savings, cycle-time reduction, headcount impact, or error-rate metrics were captured in any NextStep entry or Salesforce field. The business case template and ROI calculator were requested by Luke on 30 Oct 2025 but there is no evidence Zycus delivered it or that Luke completed it. The pain is confirmed but unquantified, which weakens the urgency and CFO sponsorship. Quantifying the value case with Luke and securing CFO sign-off on the ROI is the immediate next move.

champion.narrative: Luke Cerra is the developing champion. He initiated contact with Zycus on 6 Nov 2025 after initially being 'down the road with Coupa,' attended a Zycus demo, joined the 18 Nov 2025 Zycus webinar, and presented both Coupa and Zycus to the CFO on 24 Nov 2025 with a positive outcome ('very happy with an S2P option'). Luke requested pricing, business case materials, a contract, and a design session, which are signs of active engagement and intent. However, Luke is an Influencer, not a Decision Maker or Economic Buyer. He has no documented access to budget authority or final contract approval, and he has not introduced Zycus to the CFO, CIO, CPO, or Head of IT. His advocacy has cooled significantly: the last contact was 25 Feb 2026 (122 days ago), and his 9 Feb 2026 update indicated the project was on hold for 6 months due to the Pronto upgrade delay. There is no evidence Luke is actively pushing Zycus internally, blocking Coupa, or driving the agenda. If Luke has deprioritized or if Coupa has reached the CFO and CIO directly, Zycus is at risk of being outflanked.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the primary named competitor and is actively in the deal. Luke stated on 6 Nov 2025 that Australian Homeware was 'down the road with Coupa' before opening the door to Zycus, and on 24 Nov 2025 that both Coupa and Zycus would be considered after the Pronto upgrade. Coupa's positioning is unknown (no intelligence on their pricing, champion, CFO access, or Pronto integration story), but the fact that Luke mentioned Coupa first and repeatedly suggests they may have incumbency or stronger relationships. Status quo is also a competitor: the Pronto ERP upgrade dependency means the buyer may choose to delay or abandon the S2P project if the new Pronto version includes embedded procurement or compliance features that reduce the S2P business case urgency. The wedge for Zycus is the Merlin/Agentic AI capability (included in Products scope) and the full S2P suite breadth (iContract, iSource, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin Intake, ANA). However, Zycus has not yet positioned Merlin or AI as a differentiator in any recorded interaction, and the AI appetite is unknown (no AIS score).

No RFP launched. No multi-threading. No technical or commercial diligence. Competing against Coupa with no intelligence. Stage Formal Evaluation is a misnomer; deal is stalled, not actively being worked. High risk of being deprioritized or lost.

north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2027-02-24 is 242 days out. Deal created 14 Oct 2025, 257 days old. In Formal Evaluation stage since at least Feb 2026 (122+ days in stage). Last buyer touch 25 Feb 2026. NextStep log shows buyer stall: 9 Feb 2026 Luke met with CPO, Pronto upgrade taking much longer than expected, procurement review delayed 6 months. That 6-month window (Aug 2026) has now passed with zero buyer contact. No field history shows stage progression since creation. With 122 days of silence, zero multi-threading to power roles, and a technical dependency (Pronto upgrade + integration approval from IT/CIO) unresolved, the deal is not moving at the required pace. Required pace to close Feb 2027 from a genuine Formal Evaluation would be shortlisting by Sep 2026, vendor selection Oct/Nov 2026, contracting Dec 2026-Jan 2027. Currently 4 months behind that timeline and relationship is cold.

flags: Re-engage Luke Cerra immediately (121 days dark since last contact). The 9 Feb Next Step said they would reach out when ready, but 4 months of silence is too long. Risk: Luke has either deprioritized, gone with Coupa, or the project has stalled. Action: request an executive-level conversation (deal owner's manager to CFO) to validate the business case and confirm Zycus is still under consideration, and a technical deep-dive with Jack to prove Pronto API integration is low-risk.

Position against the Pronto upgrade dependency: the 9 Feb Next Step shows the Pronto upgrade is the blocker and Luke noted the new Pronto version includes embedded compliance modules, which could weaken the S2P business case. Risk: if the buyer sees Pronto as good enough, the external S2P need evaporates. Action: research the new Pronto version (as committed 30 Oct 2025), document what Pronto does NOT cover (sourcing, contracts, supplier risk, intake, AI-powered analytics, timber compliance workflows), and position Zycus as the procurement layer that complements (not competes with) Pronto finance/ERP. Offer a phased rollout that decouples from the Pronto timeline.

Single-threaded through Luke Cerra (Influencer) with zero contact to CFO, CIO, CPO, or Head of IT who control budget and integration approval. 122 days dark with no buyer engagement since 25 Feb 2026. Economic Buyer unmapped and unengaged. No champion with power. Technical dependency (Pronto integration) unvalidated. Pipeline forecast is indefensible: deal is on hold, not actively being worked.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the primary competitor, with incumbency advantage (Luke stated they were 'down the road with Coupa' before Zycus entered). Both vendors presented to CFO on 24 Nov 2025 with positive reception. Coupa's current positioning, pricing, and CFO/CIO access are unknown after 122 days of silence. Zycus differentiation: full S2P suite breadth (iContract, iSource, eProcurement, eInvoicing) plus Merlin/Agentic AI (included in Products scope but not yet positioned). Status quo risk is high: Pronto upgrade delay and potential embedded procurement features in new Pronto version could reduce S2P urgency. How we win: re-engage Luke, quantify ROI to secure CFO sponsorship, demonstrate Pronto integration to neutralize IT/CIO concerns, and position Merlin AI as the strategic differentiator Coupa cannot match.

• Across 1,153 chars of NextStep covering eight dated entries from Nov 2025 to Feb 2026 ‚Äî including CPO Luke's CFO presentation, Pronto upgrade wait, RFP template ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic or GenAI despite Merlin Intake, Agentic AI and ANA being in product scope. Per the 9 Feb 2026 entry: 'met with CPO, upgrade of Pronto is taking much longer than expected. Looking at reviewing Procurement in 6 months time.'
• Per the 24 Nov 2025 entry: 'Spoke to Luke, he presented to the CFO. Very happy with an S2P option. Coupa and Zycus are going to be considered. First they need to upgrade Pronto, and then clean their data in Pronto then will seek further info from both vendors. Will reach out at the back early 2026.' On 6 Nov 2025: 'We did a demo, and overview- he is joining our event online PlAn on 18th. Presenting us and Coupa to CFO in 1 week for further actions internally.' Coupa is the competitive comparison; Pronto ERP upgrade is the gating dependency.
• Confidence is Medium. Aust Homeware has the full AI loadout (Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) and CPO Luke is engaged with the CFO, but the deal is gated by Pronto ERP upgrade timing and the competitive frame is Coupa-vs-Zycus on core S2P. With zero AI keyword across 8 entries and the deal pushed to a 6-month review, score 4 reflects nominal AI scope without buyer AI engagement.
